2605.15877.title: Shapley Neuron Values for Continual Learning: Which Neurons Matter Most?
2605.15877.paper_summary: Continual learning enables neural networks to learn tasks sequentially without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. However, neural networks suffer from catastrophic forgetting, where learning new tasks degrades performance on earlier ones. We address this problem with Shapley Neuron Valuation (SNV), a principled framework that quantifies Neuron importance in continual learning, grounded in cooperative game theory. SNV selectively freezes important Neurons while keeping others plastic, enabling buffer-free continual learning without expanding architecture. Experiments on ImageNet-1k show that SNV consistently outperforms existing buffer-free methods. In particular, SNV improves accuracy by +2.88% in the class incremental learning and +6.46% in the task incremental learning scenarios compared to the second baseline.
2605.15877.paper_summary_zh: 持續學習使神經網絡能夠按順序學習任務，而不會忘記先前獲得的知識。<lb/>然而，神經網絡面臨著災難性遺忘的問題，即學習新任務會降低早期任務的性能。<lb/>我們通過 Shapley Neuron Valuation (SNV) 來解決這個問題，這是一個基於合作博弈論的原則性框架，用於量化持續學習中神經元的重要性。<lb/>SNV 有選擇性地凍結重要的神經元，同時保持其他神經元的可塑性，實現無緩衝區的持續學習，而不擴展架構。<lb/>在 ImageNet-1k 上的實驗顯示，SNV 始終優於現有的無緩衝區方法。<lb/>特別是，與第二基線相比，SNV 在類增量學習中提高了 +2.88% 的準確率，在任務增量學習場景中提高了 +6.46% 的準確率。
2605.15877.author: Mohammad Ali Vahedifar et.al.
2605.15877.authors: Mohammad Ali Vahedifar, Abhisek Ray, Qi Zhang
